sections.heading: Executive Structural Reading
sections.intent_line: Frame the case-level divergence in one line.
sections.conclusion_line: Conclusion: the live divergence is continuity protection versus repositioning burden concentration.
sections.support_line: The key split sits in proof burden timing, not in generic opportunity narrative.
sections.basis_line: Assessment basis: comparative structural exposure and commitment thresholds.

items: Comparative implication: decision quality depends on validating burden concentration before irreversible commitment.

sections.heading: External Comparative Snapshot
sections.intent_line: Surface path-level asymmetry with table-first readability.
sections.conclusion_line: Conclusion: Option A carries lower conversion burden; Option B carries higher repositioning demand with higher upside optionality.
sections.support_line: The table is primary; reading and implication lines remain secondary and compact.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: transferability, timing exposure, and conversion burden.

items: Comparative reading: burden concentration diverges more by execution load than by declared preference.
Comparative implication: define proof thresholds per path before selecting commitment timing.

rows.option_a: Higher continuity protection with lower immediate transition friction
rows.option_b: Lower continuity protection during initial conversion period

rows.option_a: Moderate conversion burden with clearer operating conditions
rows.option_b: High conversion burden with tighter proof requirements

rows.option_a: More timing slack before irreversible commitment
rows.option_b: Higher timing exposure with narrower defensibility window

comparative_table.legend: Table-first comparative block; interpretation lines are secondary support only.

sections.heading: Structural Risk Diagnosis
sections.intent_line: Pinpoint where fragility is concentrated by path.
sections.conclusion_line: Risk concentration is materially higher on Option B due to timing and conversion load overlap.
sections.support_line: Option A risk is steadier but still exposed to execution sequencing errors.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: overlap between external volatility and path-specific burden concentration.

items: Risk implication: burden concentration should be reduced before stronger path commitment.

sections.heading: Career Value Structure
sections.intent_line: Clarify continuity value versus repositioning value by path.
sections.conclusion_line: Option A preserves continuity value; Option B increases repositioning value potential with added proof burden.
sections.support_line: Trade-off quality depends on the defensibility of the conversion plan, not aspiration intensity.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: continuity protection versus repositioning demand.

items: Value implication: select path only after explicit downside containment boundaries are met.

sections.heading: Career Mobility Structure
sections.intent_line: Compare transferability, timing exposure, and transition friction.
sections.conclusion_line: Option B has higher mobility upside but materially higher conversion friction and timing exposure.
sections.support_line: Option A has lower upside variance but lower execution volatility.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: transferability and conversion burden under constrained timing windows.

items: Mobility implication: the higher-variance path requires earlier burden-proofing milestones.

sections.heading: Strategic Temperament
sections.intent_line: Distinguish governance demands under each path.
sections.conclusion_line: Option A demands continuity governance; Option B demands stricter proof discipline under uncertainty.
sections.support_line: Temperament fit depends on consistency of threshold execution, not confidence language.
sections.basis_line: Assessment basis: governance burden under structural pressure.

items: Temperament implication: choose the path whose proof burden discipline is realistically sustainable.

sections.heading: Decision Conditions
sections.intent_line: Define defensibility thresholds by path before commitment.
sections.conclusion_line: Commitment is defensible only when path-specific proof thresholds are met and downside containment remains intact.
sections.support_line: Condition clarity is mandatory before irreversible timing commitments.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: commitment defensibility under divergent burden profiles.

items: Condition A: continuity downside containment threshold met.
Condition B: repositioning conversion burden threshold met.
Condition C: timing exposure remains inside predefined limits.
